basic_details.job_title: JNU Guest Faculty Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) Guest Faculty Notification 2026 has been released on 30 June 2026 on the official website https://www.jnu.ac.in. A total of 2 vacancies have been announced through the JNU Guest Faculty Recruitment 2026.

qualification.education_qualification: Master's degree with 55% marks in concerned/relevant/allied subject, NET/SLET/SET or Ph.D. as per UGC Regulations 2018, M.D. (Ayurveda) with specified specialization for each post
qualification.details: Guest Faculty - Centre for Ayurveda Biology (Rasa Shastra Bhaishajya Kalpana): M.D. (Ayurveda) with specialization in Rasa Shastra Bhaishajya Kalpana from a recognized university/institute. Master's degree with 55% marks, NET/SLET/SET or Ph.D. as per UGC Regulations 2018. Guest Faculty - Centre for Ayurveda Biology (Ayurveda Samhita or Maulik Siddhant): M.D. (Ayurveda) with specialization in Ayurveda Samhita or Maulik Siddhant from a recognized university/institute. Master's degree with 55% marks, NET/SLET/SET or Ph.D. as per UGC Regulations 2018.

salary_details.details: Rs.1500/- per lecture subject to a maximum of Rs.50,000/- per month.

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for JNU Various Posts Recruitment 2026 via email should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 14-07-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

content_sections.heading: JNU Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates should download and read the JNU Jobs 2026 notification PDF carefully to check vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, and application instructions. The notification also includes important dates and selection process details. The direct download link is available in the Important Links section below. Download JNU Job 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: JNU Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Guest Faculty - Centre for Ayurveda Biology (Rasa Shastra Bhaishajya Kalpana): M.D. (Ayurveda) with specialization in Rasa Shastra Bhaishajya Kalpana from a recognized university/institute. Master's degree with 55% marks, NET/SLET/SET or Ph.D. as per UGC Regulations 2018. Guest Faculty - Centre for Ayurveda Biology (Ayurveda Samhita or Maulik Siddhant): M.D. (Ayurveda) with specialization in Ayurveda Samhita or Maulik Siddhant from a recognized university/institute. Master's degree with 55% marks, NET/SLET/SET or Ph.D. as per UGC Regulations 2018.

content_sections.heading: JNU Recruitment 2026 - Salary
content_sections.text: Rs.1500/- per lecture subject to a maximum of Rs.50,000/- per month.

rows.event: Last Date for Receipt of Applications

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Candidates who wish to apply for JNU Various Posts Recruitment 2026 via email should follow the steps below. Read the official notification carefully before applying. Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience. Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs. Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email. Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification. Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 14-07-2026. Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the JNU Guest Faculty recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application via email for the JNU Guest Faculty recruitment is 14th July 2026. Ensure your application reaches the university before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the total number of vacancies for Guest Faculty posts at JNU?
faq.answer: Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) has announced a total of 2 vacancies for the Guest Faculty positions. These positions are available in the Centre for Ayurveda Biology.

faq.question: Q3. What are the educational qualifications required for the Guest Faculty posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Master's degree with 55% marks in the relevant subject, along with NET/SLET/SET or a Ph.D. as per UGC Regulations 2018. For specific Ayurveda posts, an M.D. (Ayurveda) with the required specialization is mandatory.

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for JNU Guest Faculty recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee required for the JNU Guest Faculty recruitment. Both General/OBC and SC/ST category candidates are exempted from paying any application fee.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for applying to the Guest Faculty positions at JNU?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for candidates applying for the Guest Faculty positions at JNU is 70 years. There is no minimum age limit specified in the notification.

faq.question: Q6. How will candidates be selected for the Guest Faculty positions?
faq.answer: The selection process for the Guest Faculty positions at JNU will primarily involve an Interview. Candidates will be shortlisted based on their qualifications and performance in the interview.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary structure for the Guest Faculty at JNU?
faq.answer: The salary for Guest Faculty positions at JNU is structured as Rs. 1500/- per lecture, with a maximum monthly earning capped at Rs. 50,000/-.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Interested candidates can apply for the JNU Guest Faculty recruitment through email. You need to send your application along with necessary documents to the official email address provided by the university.

faq.question: Q9. Which departments are recruiting Guest Faculty?
faq.answer: The recruitment is for the Centre for Ayurveda Biology at JNU. Specifically, there are positions for Guest Faculty in Rasa Shastra Bhaishajya Kalpana and Ayurveda Samhita or Maulik Siddhant.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification for JNU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official notification for JNU Recruitment 2026 can be accessed through the 'Important Links' section of this article. A direct link to the PDF is provided there for your convenience.
